hawkish_phrases: inflation outlook was still too high for too long
risks to price stability as being clearly tilted to the upside
signs of a wage-profit-price spiral were emerging
policy rates would be brought to levels that were sufficiently restrictive...and would be kept at those levels for as long as necessary
dovish_phrases: accounting for elevated uncertainty about the speed and strength of monetary policy transmission
substantial ground had been covered thus far, with the Governing Council having raised rates by 350 basis points
lower energy prices, the easing of supply bottlenecks
exclusion-based and model-based measures had started to decline
weaker demand...would also lead to lower price pressures
summary: The Governing Council raised rates by 25 basis points (slowing from prior 50bp increments) with almost all members supporting the decision, while emphasizing that rates must reach sufficiently restrictive levels and remain there as underlying inflation pressures and upside risks persist.
